problem.title: Count Primes
problem.description: Given an integer n, return the number of prime numbers that are strictly less than n.
assumptions: Count primes strictly less than n.
Use Sieve of Eratosthenes for efficiency.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sieve of Eratosthenes [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Initialize a boolean array marking all numbers as prime, then for each prime p starting from 2, mark all multiples of p starting at p² as composite. Starting at p² is the key optimization — smaller multiples were already marked by earlier primes — reducing the total work to O(n log log n).
